prevalence.trend: increasing due to urbanization and pollution

incidence.source: Indian Journal of Allergy and Asthma (2020 study)

burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study 2019, adapted for India

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Symptoms including sneezing, nasal itching, rhinorrhea, and congestion lasting >1 hour on most days for >1 year; confirmed by positive skin prick test or elevated specific IgE levels (ARIA guidelines)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Adapted ARIA guidelines with emphasis on local allergens
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: First-line: Intranasal corticosteroids and oral antihistamines; allergen avoidance; immunotherapy for severe cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High air pollution in northern cities like Delhi worsens symptoms; limited access in rural areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable generic medications; growing allergy clinics in urban centers
